DTR  |  Listen to a Short Exchange
LSE-HS11-L4-1400P-HSELA1112-HJD-IFN-01 • HS English 11-12 • Citing Strong and Thorough Textual Evidence

The Garrison Ledger

Click   🎧   to listen.
Listen to a conversation between a teacher and a girl. Listen carefully. You will hear the conversation only once. After listening, you will answer a question.
Listen to the Conversation
QUESTION:
What was the teacher's lesson about?
Choose the best answer. You may change your answer before submitting.
SMART SUPPORT:
Go back to the conversation. Construct a complete academic justification. State your claim about what the teacher's lesson is about, support that claim with reasoning drawn directly from what he says the entry establishes and what it leaves uncertain, and articulate the conclusion that follows about how to use a primary source as evidence.
CA ELD PI.B.5  |  CCSS RI.11-12.1  |  HS English 11-12: Citing Strong and Thorough Textual Evidence
